meta.title: F31 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every BMW 3 Series F31 engine – N13, N20, N55, B38, B48, B58, N47, B47 and N57. Real UK costs and honest reliability data. Get free quotes from 20+ vetted BMW specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 3 Series F31 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned F31 engine typically costs £1,500–£8,000 supply only [BMW-QUOTE], depending on the engine – the N13 four-cylinder sits at £1,500–£3,000, while the N57 335d xDrive can exceed £8,000. Fitting adds £520–£1,170 at independent specialists. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which F31 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B58B30A (340i, post-2015) is widely considered BMW's finest modern engine – smooth, powerful and robust with no major failure points beyond routine cooling system maintenance. Among diesels, the B47 (post-2015) is a significant improvement over the N47. The B48 four-cylinder is also highly reliable.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 3 Series F31?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain guide failure on the N20 petrol four-cylinder (pre-2015), timing chain stretch on the N47 diesel (pre-2015), EGR cooler failure on diesel engines (N47, B47, N57), water pump and cooling system wear on B-series engines (B38, B48, B58, post-2015), and turbocharger issues on the N57 335d.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 3 Series F31 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends heavily on the engine and year. Pre-2015 N20 and N47 engines carry significant timing chain risks – avoid without documented chain work. Post-2015 B-series engines (B38, B48, B58, B47) are significantly more reliable. The N57 six-cylinder diesel is robust but requires EGR cooler attention. Choose wisely, and the F31 is a dependable estate.

mainEntity.name: Which F31 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pre-2015 N20 (320i/328i) and N47 (316d–320d) engines without documented timing chain replacement – failure can be catastrophic. The N13 316i also has timing chain concerns. If budget allows, prioritise post-2015 B-series cars.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my F31 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cold-start rattle (N20/N47 timing chain), persistent coolant loss (B-series water pump), EGR cooler warnings on diesels, or a sudden loss of power are the key warning signs. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 3 Series F31 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply the 70% BMW scrap threshold. Against a well-kept £12,000 320d, a £2,800–£5,000 N47/B47 recon is 23–42% – safe. Against a tired £6,000 example, the same recon is 47–83% – walk away unless the car is exceptional. For a £20,000 340i, a £7,000 B58 replacement is 35% – well within the safe zone.

mainEntity.name: How long do F31 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The N57 diesel six and N55/B58 petrol six are capable of 150,000+ miles with proper maintenance. The N47/B47 diesel four can achieve similar mileage with diligent chain maintenance. The N20 petrol four can also reach high mileage but requires chain guide replacement as preventative maintenance at 60,000–80,000 miles. The B-series engines (B38, B48, B58) are robust and should exceed 150,000 miles.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the F31 and the F30 saloon?

acceptedAnswer.text: The F31 is the Touring (estate) version of the sixth-generation 3 Series, sharing the same platform, engines and interior as the F30 saloon. The F31 offers additional load space and practicality, with a 495-litre boot expanding to 1,500 litres with the rear seats folded. Engine options are identical between the two body styles.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my F31 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate (visible through the windscreen) and vehicle documentation. The engine code is also stamped on the cylinder block – on B-series engines, it's on the left-hand side near the timing cover. Tell us your variant and year (pre-/post-2015 LCI) and we'll identify it – 20+ vetted BMW specialists will confirm it for free.

hero.h1: BMW 3 Series F31 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the F31 Touring – from the 1.6-litre N13 four-cylinder to the 3.0-litre N57 six-cylinder diesel and the B58 that replaced the N55. Real UK replacement costs, honest reliability data, and the timing chain concerns that defined the N20 and N47 era. Backed by real UK F31 enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED].

engineDatabase.h2: BMW 3 Series F31 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the F31 Touring – from the entry-level four-cylinder to the range-topping six-cylinder diesel.

overview.intro: The BMW F31, launched in 2012 as the Touring (estate) version of the sixth-generation 3 Series, combines the practicality of a load-lugging estate with the dynamic prowess expected of a BMW. Built on the same platform as the F30 saloon, the F31 features a 40:60 front-to-rear weight distribution and a range of turbocharged four- and six-cylinder engines. A 2015 facelift brought B-series engines and updated technology, while the range-topping 335d xDrive offered 313 hp and all-wheel-drive traction.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: BMW L7 (F30/F31/F34) • Predecessor: BMW 3 Series E91 • Successor: BMW 3 Series G21 • Sibling body styles in this generation: F30 (saloon), F34 (Gran Turismo)
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The F31 generated real UK engine enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED], with the N47/B47 diesel and N20/B48 petrol accounting for the majority of demand as these cars enter their second and third ownership cycles.

items.engine: B47D20A (post-2015, LCI) – "The B47 is a significant improvement over the N47 – timing chain issues are less prevalent, and the EGR cooler recall has addressed a known weakness. It's smoother, cleaner and more reliable than its predecessor."
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want diesel economy without the N47's timing chain anxiety.

items.engine: B58B30A (340i, post-2015) – "The B58 straight-six is BMW's finest modern engine – 326 hp of silky-smooth power with none of the N55's earlier weaknesses. Water pump and cooling system are the only real demands; this engine is widely considered bulletproof."
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want effortless six-cylinder power with modern reliability.

items.engine: N20B20 (320i/328i, pre-2015) – "The N20 four-cylinder carries a significant timing chain guide weakness – failure can occur as early as 60,000 miles, leading to a £4,000+ engine replacement. Cold-start rattle is the warning sign."
items.whoItsFor: only buyers who have confirmed chain guide replacement or are budgeting for it.

items.engine: N57D30B (335d xDrive) – "The triple-turbo diesel is a complex piece of engineering – turbocharger failure and EGR cooler issues can lead to bills exceeding £5,000. The all-wheel-drive system adds further complexity."
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want diesel performance and accept the associated running costs.

items.engine: B48B20A (320i, post-2015) – "The B48 four-cylinder delivers 184 hp with none of the N20's timing chain anxiety. It's smoother, more efficient and more reliable than its predecessor – the sweet spot of the F31 petrol range."
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want modern four-cylinder reliability without the N20 gamble.

items.engine: B38B15 (318i, post-2015) – "The 1.5-litre three-cylinder is often dismissed as underpowered, but it delivers 136 hp with excellent economy and a characterful exhaust note. It's the cheapest F31 to run and maintain."
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want F31 estate practicality with minimal running costs.

rows.ownershipVerdict: Watch – Prince engine; timing chain concerns.
High Risk – chain failure is a grenade.
Solid – robust six, minor cooling issues.
Best Petrol – no chain issues, reliable.
Best Six – BMW's finest modern engine.
Watch – chain issues less common than N47 in E90 but still present.
Best Diesel – improved chain design, reliable.

economicsRule.title: The F31 rule
economicsRule.text: A £2,800–£5,000 N47/B47 recon against a £6,000–£15,000 F31 sits at 19–83% of the car's value. On a sub-£8,000 diesel F31, that brushes our 70% BMW threshold – we'll honestly tell you to walk away unless the car has documented chain work. Against a £12,000+ low-mileage example, the same recon drops to a routine 23–42% – a repair decision, not a financial one. The B58 replacement at £7,000 is well within the value of a £20,000 340i.

keyTakeaways.question: Which F31 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: B38B15 (318i) – cheapest entry, lowest insurance, simple three-cylinder.

keyTakeaways.question: Which F31 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: N57D30B (335d) – triple-turbo complexity and premium running costs.

keyTakeaways.question: Which F31 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: B48B20 (320i/330i) – the sweet spot between performance and running costs.

problems.title: N20 Timing Chain Guide Failure
problems.rootCause: The plastic timing chain guides degrade and break, allowing the chain to slacken and skip teeth. Cold-start rattle for 1–2 seconds is the classic early warning. If ignored, the chain can snap, causing piston-to-valve contact and destroying the engine.
problems.recommendation: If you own a pre-2015 N20-powered F31, treat the timing chain as an urgent inspection item – £800–£1,500 now is dramatically cheaper than the £2,000+ replacement that follows a failure. Any cold-start rattle should be investigated immediately.

cta.label: Compare N20 engine prices for your BMW 3 Series F31 →

problems.title: N47 Timing Chain Stretch
problems.rootCause: The rear-mounted timing chain on the N47 diesel can stretch and wear the guides – a known issue carried forward from the E90 generation. Cold-start rattle is the warning sign, and failure leads to engine destruction.
problems.recommendation: If you're considering a pre-2015 diesel F31, budget for timing chain replacement if not already documented – £800–£1,500 now is cheap insurance against a £2,800+ replacement. Post-2015 B47 engines have improved chain design.

cta.label: Compare N47 engine prices for your BMW 3 Series F31 →

problems.title: EGR Cooler Failure (Diesel Engines)
problems.affectedModels: F31 diesel variants (N47, B47, N57), particularly pre-2017 examples.
problems.rootCause: The EGR cooler can develop internal coolant leaks, leading to coolant loss and potential engine damage. BMW has issued recalls for some variants.
problems.recommendation: Check if your F31 diesel is covered by the EGR cooler recall – if not, have the cooler inspected at every service. A £400–£800 repair is far cheaper than the £2,800+ replacement that follows an overheat.

cta.label: Compare EGR cooler repair quotes for your BMW 3 Series F31 →

problems.title: B38/B48/B58 Water Pump & Cooling System
problems.affectedModels: F31 petrol variants with B-series engines (2015–2019).
problems.rootCause: The plastic water pump impeller and thermostat housing can degrade with heat cycling – a known characteristic of BMW's B-series engines. Coolant leaks are the first sign.
problems.recommendation: At the first sign of coolant loss, have the water pump and thermostat housing inspected – a £300–£600 repair is cheap insurance against a £1,800+ replacement.

cta.label: Compare B38/B48/B58 engine prices for your BMW 3 Series F31 →

replacementCosts.h2: F31 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are F31 only. The F30 saloon and F34 Gran Turismo share most of these engines but are covered on their own Generation Pages, since body style affects boot space and weight rather than engine fitment. The 335d xDrive was the only diesel offered with all-wheel drive in the F31 range.
